Buzzard Survival

Meinolf Ottensmann

This document details all required steps to repeat analyses presented within our manuscript.

Load required Packages

rm(list = ls())
library(tidyverse)
library(magrittr)
library(patchwork)
library(RMark)
library(sjPlot)
library(sjlabelled)
library(sjmisc)
library(lme4)
library(forestmodel)

Load raw data

NAO indices

Here, we use NAO indices that are based on the principal component approach 1 (Hurrel, 2003).

nao <- read.table("data/nao.txt", header = T) %>% 
  mutate(time = as.factor(year))

buzzard data

This analysis is based on data from a longterm study on common buzzards Buteo buteo comprising 2499 individuals that were ringed as nestlings and individually marked with wingtags in the period 2007 to 2019. Note, due to logistic constraints there is no information on Leucocytozoon infections available for the cohort of 2008 and thus individuals from that cohort are omitted from analyses. There are two datasets, ringing_data containing morphometric measures of all individuals used for this analysis and resighting_data detailing resightings for these individuals.

ringing_data <- as.data.frame(read_csv("data/ringing_data.csv")) 
resighting_data <- as.data.frame(read_csv("data/resighting_data.csv"))
## age distribution
hist(ringing_data$Age_days)

quantile(ringing_data$Age_days, c(0.025, 0.975))
##     2.5%    97.5% 
## 20.12795 39.29352
mean(ringing_data$Age_days)
## [1] 29.15691

Statistical Analysis

Burnham model (Live & dead encounters)

We model survival probabilites using combined dead and live encounter events using the Burnham model in MARK.

Based on ringing data and resightings of individuals, we assemble combined live-dead encounter histories for all individuals using the function create.ld. This functions generates a vector of the form LDLDLD, where L codes live encounters and D dead recoveries respectively. Together, each LD pair codes for one interval of the study period.

## function to derive encounter histories
create.ld <- function(start, end, ringing_data, resights) {
  ## Identify all individuals 
  individuals <- ringing_data$Ring %>%
    unique()
  
  ## define Study periods based on start and end years
  years <- c(start, end)
  periods <- rep(seq(start, end), each = 1)

  ## create matrix 
  ## rows: Individuals
  ## cols: 2 Periods per year with Live and Dead encounters respectively
  mat <- matrix(0, nrow = length(individuals), ncol = length(periods)*2) %>% 
    set_colnames(paste0(rep(periods, each = 2), c("L", "D"))) %>% 
    set_rownames(individuals)
  
  ## code year of capture as 1,
  ## Code sightings in subsequent intervals by 1 too
  for (x in individuals) {
    year.ringed <- filter(ringing_data, Ring == x)[["Year"]][1]
      ## code birth year in living encounters
      mat[rownames(mat) == x, colnames(mat) == paste0(year.ringed, "L")] <- 1  

      ## get resights and assign to sampling instances. As years are defined
      ## by the period 01.04 to 31.03, the first three month of each year count 
      ## for the preceding interval.
      year.resighted <- 
        filter(resights, Ring == x)[,c("Year", "Month")] %>% 
        mutate(period = case_when(
          Month == "01" ~ paste0(as.numeric(Year) - 1 , "L"),
          Month == "02" ~ paste0(as.numeric(Year) - 1 , "L"),
          Month == "03" ~ paste0(as.numeric(Year) - 1 , "L"),
          Month == "04" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "05" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "06" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "07" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "08" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "09" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "10" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "11" ~ paste0(Year, "L"),
          Month == "12" ~ paste0(Year, "L")
        ))

        ## write resights to matrix 
  for (col in year.resighted$period) {
    mat[rownames(mat) == x, colnames(mat) == col] <- 1
  }
  
  ## check if reported as dead
  death <- filter(ringing_data, Ring == x)[,c("Dead", "DateDeath")][1,]
  if (!is.na(death$Dead)) {
    col <- paste0(substr(death$DateDeath, 1, 4),"D")
    mat[rownames(mat) == x, colnames(mat) == col] <- 1
  }
  
}

## coerce to character vector
return(apply(mat[,], 1, function(x) paste0(x, collapse = "")) %>% 
         as.character())

}

Apply the function to the data:

## add resighting histories to the ringing data
df <- data.frame(
  filter(ringing_data, !is.na(Lprev)),
  ch =  create.ld(start = 2007, # first year
                  end = 2020, # last year
                  ringing_data = ringing_data,
                  resights = resighting_data)) %>% 
  mutate(ch = as.character(ch)) %>% 
  mutate(Sex = as.factor(Sex)) %>% 
  mutate(Hatch = scale(Hatch)) %>% 
  mutate(Morh = case_when(
    Morph == "2" ~ "Dark",
    Morph == "3" ~ "Intermediate",
    Morph == "4" ~ "Light"
  )) %>% 
  subset(., 
         select = -c(Ring, Rank, Hatch, Lbinom, Dead, DateDeath, CauseDeath))

## process the data for RMark
dp  <-  process.data(df, model = "Burnham",
                     begin.time = 2007, groups = "Sex")

## create design data
ddl <-  make.design.data(dp)

## Add design data for components 
## Survival
ddl$S <- ddl$S %>% 
  mutate(age = as.numeric(as.character(age))) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = 
           case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age == 1 ~ "CY2",
             age == 2 ~ "CY3",
             age >= 3 ~ "CY4plus"
           )) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass2 = case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age >= 1 ~ "CY2plus"
           ))

## collect variables which are year but not individual specific
covars <- left_join(data.frame(time = ddl$S$time), nao, "time")

## extend design data with covariates
ddl$S$djfm <- covars$djfm ## Winter NAO index

## Site-Fidelity
ddl$F <- ddl$F %>% 
  mutate(age = as.numeric(as.character(age))) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = 
           case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age == 1 ~ "CY2",
             age == 2 ~ "CY3",
             age >= 3 ~ "CY4plus"
           )) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass2 = case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age >= 1 ~ "CY2plus"
           ))

## recovery chances
ddl$r <- ddl$r %>%
  mutate(age = as.numeric(as.character(age))) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = 
           case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age == 1 ~ "CY2",
             age == 2 ~ "CY3",
             age >= 3 ~ "CY4plus"
           )) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass2 = case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age >= 1 ~ "CY2plus"
           ))

## resight probability
ddl$p <- ddl$p %>% 
  mutate(age = as.numeric(as.character(age))) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = 
           case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age == 1 ~ "CY2",
             age == 2 ~ "CY3",
             age >= 3 ~ "CY4plus"
           )) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass2 = case_when(
             age == 0 ~ "CY1",
             age >= 1 ~ "CY2plus"
           ))
## Goodness of fit using RELEASE
release.gof(dp)
##       Chi.square df      P
## TEST2    22.2940 14 0.0728
## TEST3    13.0634 15 0.5974
## Total    35.3575 29 0.1930
rm(covars); rm(nao);

Bootstrapping Global model

The code below is used to implement a parametric bootstrapping to calculate median \(\hat{C}\) based in function defined in the script R/bootstrapping_functions.R

## not run, output is saved
source("R/bootstrapping.R")
## run global model to get parameter estimates
global.model <- mark(dp, ddl, silent = T, output = F,
                     model.parameters = list(
S = list(formula = ~ time*Sex), 
p = list(formula = ~ time*Sex),
F = list(formula = ~ time*Sex),
r = list(formula = ~ time*Sex)),
model = 'Burnham')

## get model estimates/effects
params <- global.model$results$real

## get parameter names
components <- lapply(row.names(params), function(x) {
  stringr::str_split(x, pattern = " ")[[1]]
}) %>% 
  do.call("rbind",.) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  set_colnames(c("Estimate", "Group", "Cohort", "a", "t"))

## extract sex-specific components
S.female <-   
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "S" & components$Group == "gFemale")]
S.male <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "S" & components$Group == "gMale")] 
p.female <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "p" & components$Group == "gFemale")]
p.male <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "p" & components$Group == "gMale")]
F.female <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "F" & components$Group == "gFemale")]
F.male <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "F" & components$Group == "gMale")]
r.male <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "r" & components$Group == "gMale")]
r.female <-
  params$estimate[which(components$Estimate == "r" & components$Group == "gFemale")]

## conduct simulations using parametric bs for both models
sim.out <- sims.burnham(
  S.fem = S.female, 
  S.mal = S.male,
  p.fem = p.female,
  p.mal = p.male,
  F.fem = F.female,
  F.mal = F.male,
  r.fem = r.female,
  r.mal = r.male,
  marked =  Marked.burnham(data = df,
                   n.occasions = nchar(df$ch[1])/2,
                   groups = c("Female","Male"),
                   group.col = "Sex"),
  group.col = "Sex", reps = 9, 
  model.parameters = list(
S = list(formula = ~ time*sex), 
p = list(formula = ~ time*sex),
F = list(formula = ~ time*sex),
r = list(formula = ~ time*sex)))

save(sim.out, file = "data/sim.out.RData")
c.hat <- global.model$results$deviance/sim.out$deviance.mean
save(c.hat, file = "data/c.hat.RData")
cleanup(ask = F)

Candidate models

The code below implements all candidate models. As the execution is computationally extensive the code is not executed by default but the output is provided within this repository.

## not run, output is saved 
## clear working space
#rm(list = c("ringing_data", "resighting_data", "df"))

do_analysis = function(dp, ddl, merge = F, suffix = NULL) {
  
  ## AGE4
  ## ###########################################################################
  S.2 = list(formula = ~ ageclass + Sex)
  S.2x = list(formula = ~ ageclass)
  
  S.3 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + Sex)
  S.3x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond)

  S.4 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + Sex)
  S.4x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm)
  
  # S.5 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Hatch + Sex)
  # S.5x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Hatch)

  S.9 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev + Sex + Morph)
  S.9x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev + Morph)
  
  S.5 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev + Sex )
  S.5x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev)
  
  S.11 = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + Morph + Sex)
  S.11x = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + Morph)
  
  S.8 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  S.8x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev + Morph)
  
  S.13 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev + Sex)
  S.13x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev)
  
  S.12 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + Sex)
  S.12x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto)
  
  S.15 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Lprev + Sex)
  S.15x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Lprev)

  # S.13 = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + ageclass * Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  # S.13x = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + ageclass * Lprev + Morph)
  
  # S.15 = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + Morph + Sex)
  # S.15x = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + Morph)
  # 
  # S.12 = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + ageclass * Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  # S.12x = list(formula = ~ageclass * cond + ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + ageclass * Lprev + Morph)
  
  S.3b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + Sex)
  S.3bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond)
  
  S.4b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + djfm + Sex)
  S.4bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + djfm)
  
  # S.5b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + djfm + Hatch + Sex)
  # S.5bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + djfm + Hatch)
  
  S.9b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + Ekto + Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  S.9bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + Ekto + Lprev + Morph)
  
  S.5b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + Ekto + Lprev + Sex)
  S.5bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + Ekto + Lprev)
  
  S.11b = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + djfm  + Ekto  + Morph + Sex)
  S.11bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + djfm  + Ekto  + Morph)
  
  S.13b = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + djfm  + Ekto + Sex)
  S.13bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + djfm  + Ekto)
  
  S.8b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + djfm + Ekto + Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  S.8bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + cond + djfm + Ekto + Lprev + Morph)
  
  # S.13b = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + Ekto + Hatch + Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  # S.13bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + Ekto + Hatch + Lprev + Morph)
  
  # S.15b = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + djfm + Ekto + Hatch + Morph + Sex)
  # S.15bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + cond + djfm + Ekto + Hatch + Morph)
  
  S.6 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * djfm + Sex)
  S.6x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * djfm)
  
  # S.7 = list(formula = ~ ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Hatch + Sex)
  # S.7x = list(formula = ~ ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Hatch)
  
  S.10 = list(formula = ~ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev +  Morph + Sex)
  S.10x = list(formula = ~ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Lprev +  Morph)
  
  # S.14 = list(formula = ~ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + ageclass * Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  # S.14x = list(formula = ~ageclass * djfm + ageclass * Ekto + ageclass * Hatch + ageclass * Lprev + Morph)
  
  S.6b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + djfm + Sex)
  S.6bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + djfm)
  
  # S.7b = list(formula = ~ ageclass + djfm + Hatch + Sex)
  # S.7bz = list(formula = ~ ageclass + djfm + Hatch)
  
  S.10b = list(formula = ~ageclass + djfm + Ekto + Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  S.10bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + djfm + Ekto + Lprev + Morph)
  
  S.14b = list(formula = ~ageclass + djfm + Ekto + Lprev + Sex)
  S.14bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + djfm + Ekto + Lprev)
  
  # S.14b = list(formula = ~ageclass + djfm + Ekto + Hatch + Lprev + Morph + Sex)
  # S.14bz = list(formula = ~ageclass + djfm + Ekto + Hatch + Lprev + Morph)
  
  #p.dot = list(formula = ~ 1)
  p.age = list(formula = ~ ageclass)
  #p.age2 = list(formula = ~ ageclass2)
  p.time = list(formula = ~ time)
  p.time.age = list(formula = ~ time + ageclass)
  #p.time.age2 = list(formula = ~ time + ageclass2)

  F.dot = list(formula = ~ 1)
  F.age = list(formula = ~ ageclass)
  F.age.sex = list(formula = ~ ageclass + Sex)
  #F.age2 = list(formula = ~ ageclass2)
  #F.age2.sex = list(formula = ~ ageclass2 + Sex)
  F.Sex = list(formula = ~ Sex)
  
  #r.dot = list(formula = ~ 1)
  #r.age = list(formula = ~ ageclass)
  r.age2 = list(formula = ~ ageclass2)
  
  cml = create.model.list(model = "Burnham")
  save(cml, file = "data/cml.temp.RData")
  
  # only run new models 
  if (isTRUE(merge) & !is.null(suffix)) {
  load(paste0("cml.", suffix, ".RData"))  
    cml <- anti_join(cml, cml.old)
  }
  
  ## split task 
  split <- c(round(seq(0, nrow(cml), length.out = 10)))

  for (i in 1:(length(split)-1)) {
  # run all models and return as a list with class 'marklist'
   pb <- utils::txtProgressBar(min = 1, max = length(split) -1,
                               style = 3, char = "+", width = 80)
   utils::setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)

   results = mark.wrapper.parallel(cml[(split[i] + 1):split[i + 1],],
                                   data = dp,
                                   ddl = ddl,
                                   output = F,
                                   silent = T,
                                   cpus = 4)
   save(results, file = paste0("data/results", i, ".RData"))
   rm(results)
  }
  
  ## merge results
  indices <- data.frame(first = split[1:9] + 1,
            last = split[2:10],
            n = diff(split)
  )
  
  cat("Merge results ... \n")
  cat("1 out of 10 \n")
  load("data/results1.RData"); results1 <- results; rm(results)
  
  cat("2 out of 10 \n")
  load("data/results2.RData"); results2 <- results; rm(results)
  names(results2)[1:indices$n[2]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[2]:indices$last[2])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results1, results2); rm(results1); rm(results2)
  
  cat("3 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results3.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[3]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[3]:indices$last[3])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  cat("4 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results4.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[4]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[4]:indices$last[4])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  cat("5 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results5.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[5]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[5]:indices$last[5])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  cat("6 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results6.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[6]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[6]:indices$last[6])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  cat("7 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results7.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[7]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[7]:indices$last[7])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  cat("8 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results8.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[8]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[8]:indices$last[8])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  cat("9 out of 10\n")
  load("data/results9.RData")
  names(results)[1:indices$n[9]] <-  paste0("model", indices$first[9]:indices$last[9])
  results.comb <- merge.mark(results.comb, results); rm(results)
  
  return(results.comb)
}

# if (!is.null(suffix)) {
# load(paste0("data/models.", suffix, ".RData"))
# assign(paste0("models.old"), models)
# }
models <- do_analysis(dp = dp, ddl = ddl, merge = F, suffix = NULL)

## only temp
#rm(ddl); rm(dp)

# if (!is.null(suffix)) {
# models <- merge.mark(models, models.old)
# }

## find duplicated models
# mt <- model.table(models, sort = F)
# to.remove <- which(duplicated(mt$model))
# ## remove 
# models <- remove.mark(
#   marklist = models,
#   model.numbers = to.remove)

## adjust for overdispersion using c-hat
load("data/c.hat.RData")
models.adj <-  adjust.chat(model.list = models, chat = c.hat) 

## Select supported models
supported.models <- remove.mark(
  marklist = models.adj,
  model.numbers = which(model.table(models.adj, sort = F)[["DeltaQAICc"]] > 10))

suffix <- Sys.time() %>% 
  as.character() %>% 
  stringr::str_remove(., " ") %>% 
  gsub(pattern = ":", replacement = "-", x = .)

## save cml object
load("data/cml.temp.RData")
assign(paste0("cml.old"), cml)
save(cml.old, file = paste0("cml.", suffix, ".RData"))

save(suffix, file = paste0("data/",suffix,".RData"))
save(models, file = paste0("data/models.", suffix, ".RData"))
save(models.adj, file = paste0("data/models.adj.", suffix, ".RData"))
save(supported.models, file = paste0("data/supported.models.", suffix, ".RData"))
cleanup(ask = F)

Results

As the execution of the models is time consuming, we provide the captured output as part of the repository

## load previously saved results
suffix <- "2020-10-1901-29-29"
## all models
#load(paste0("data/models.", suffix, ".RData"))
## models with adjusted deviance
load(paste0("data/models.adj.", suffix, ".RData"))
## models within DeltaQAICc <= 10 of the top ranking
load(paste0("data/supported.models.", suffix, ".RData"))

In total we evaluated 528models, of which 84 where supported to a variable extend by the data (i.e. within \(\Delta QAIC_c <= 10\) of the top model)

model.table <-supported.models$model.table[,c("model", "DeltaQAICc", "weight", "npar", "QDeviance")]
readr::write_csv(model.table, "data/supported.models.csv")

model.table <-models.adj$model.table[,c("model", "DeltaQAICc", "weight", "npar", "QDeviance")] 
readr::write_csv(model.table, "data/adjusted.models.csv")

Model averaging

## Model average Survival estimates (S) 
S.mod.avg <- model.average(
  remove.mark(
    marklist = supported.models,
    model.numbers = which(model.table(supported.models, sort = F)[["DeltaQAICc"]] > 7)),
  parameter = "S", drop = T, vcv = T)[["estimates"]] %>% 
  subset(., select = c(estimate, se, lcl, ucl, age, Sex))

## Model average Fidelity estimates (F)
F.mod.avg <- model.average(supported.models, parameter = "F", vcv = T, drop = T)$estimates %>% 
  subset(., select = (c(estimate, se, lcl, ucl, time, age, Sex))) %>% 
  unique.data.frame() 

## Model average recovery estimates (r)
r.mod.avg <- model.average(supported.models, parameter = "r", vcv = T, drop = T)$estimates %>% 
  subset(., select = (c(estimate, se, lcl, ucl, time, age, Sex))) %>% 
  unique.data.frame() 

## Model average resighting estimates (p)
p.mod.avg <- model.average(supported.models, parameter = "p", vcv = T, drop = T)$estimates %>% 
  subset(., select = (c(estimate, se, lcl, ucl, time, age))) %>% 
  unique.data.frame()

Survival probabilites

Mean estimates for age and sex combinations over time

estimates <- S.mod.avg %>% 
  mutate(AgeGroup = case_when(
    age == 0 ~ "CY1",
    age == 1 ~ "CY2",
    age == 2 ~ "CY3",
    age %in% 3:13 ~ "CY4+"
  ))

(DBChecks::summary_stats(estimates,
                        measurevar = "estimate",
                        groupvars = c("Sex", "AgeGroup")))
##      Sex AgeGroup  N  estimate         sd        q1        q3       lci
## 1 Female      CY1 14 0.4806288 0.04881275 0.4598833 0.5074017 0.3804669
## 2 Female      CY2 13 0.5776091 0.12278364 0.5297282 0.6426662 0.3329832
## 3 Female      CY3 12 0.7432106 0.09985097 0.7103014 0.8040899 0.5416718
## 4 Female     CY4+ 66 0.8838237 0.07880066 0.8990569 0.9393861 0.6734455
## 5   Male      CY1 14 0.4208724 0.04706769 0.4001735 0.4466288 0.3251022
## 6   Male      CY2 13 0.5207625 0.12240708 0.4688473 0.5849760 0.2816397
## 7   Male      CY3 12 0.6965328 0.10822210 0.6578343 0.7627914 0.4812215
## 8   Male     CY4+ 66 0.8578063 0.08969585 0.8740758 0.9233145 0.6187391
##         uci         se
## 1 0.5338012 0.01304576
## 2 0.7078213 0.03405406
## 3 0.8402500 0.02882449
## 4 0.9427201 0.00969969
## 5 0.4729550 0.01257937
## 6 0.6550719 0.03394962
## 7 0.8045883 0.03124103
## 8 0.9274362 0.01104080
Age
## derive ageclass-specfic annual survival estimates
df.S.age <- lapply(rownames(S.mod.avg), function(row) {
  x <- stringr::str_split(row, " ")[[1]]
  data.frame(parameter = x[1],
             cohort = substr(x[3], 2, nchar(x[3])),
             year = as.numeric(substr(x[5], 2, nchar(x[5]))))
}) %>% 
  do.call("rbind",.) %>% 
  cbind(S.mod.avg) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = case_when(
    age == 0 ~ 'CY1',
    age == 1 ~ 'CY2',
    age == 2 ~ 'CY3',
    age %in% 3:13 ~ 'CY4plus' ## 3:13 are identical 
  )) 

p.age <- 
  ggplot(filter(df.S.age, age %in% 0:3), aes(x = year, y = estimate, group = age)) +
  geom_line(lty = "dotted", aes(col = age)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - se, ymax = estimate +se,  color = age), width = 0, size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(col = age, shape = age), size = 2) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    # axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    # axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    # axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "Black"),
    legend.margin = margin(c(1,1,1,1)),
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.position = c(0.75, 0.06),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "PuOr", 
                     labels = c("CY1", "CY2", "CY3", "CY4+"),
                     name = "") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(1, 2, 5, 9),
                     labels = c("CY1", "CY2", "CY3", "CY4+"),
                     name = "") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.1, 1),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, .2)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2007, 2020, 1),
                     limits = c(2007, 2020)) +
  xlab("Year") +
  ylab("Suvival probability \U03D5 [mean \U00B1 SE]") + 
  facet_wrap(~Sex, ncol = 1, strip.position = "top")
p.age

ggsave("plots/Fig_1_AgeEffect.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in")
NAO
djfm.values = ddl$S$djfm %>% unique()

## Extract get par.index of interest
index.meta <- data.frame(
  ageclass = rep(c("CY1", "CY2", "CY3", "CY4plus"), 1)) %>%   
  ## add par.index
  left_join(., ddl$S[,c("par.index", "ageclass", "djfm")]) %>% 
  ## code additional vars
  mutate(dummy = paste0(djfm, ageclass)) 

## simplify, one par.index for each dummy var.
model.indices <- sapply(unique(index.meta$dummy), function(x) {
  ## return first row matching the query
  index.meta[index.meta$dummy == x,"par.index"][1]
}) %>%
  unlist()

index.meta <- index.meta[index.meta$par.index %in% model.indices,]

pred.djfm <- covariate.predictions(
  model = supported.models, 
  data = data.frame(
    djfm = djfm.values), 
  indices = index.meta$par.index)[["estimates"]]

## extend df with grouping variables
pred.model <- left_join(
  pred.djfm,
  index.meta[,c("par.index", "ageclass")],
  by = "par.index") %>% 
  mutate(Group = ageclass)

pred.model <- left_join(
  index.meta,
  pred.model[,c("par.index", "estimate", "lcl", "ucl", "se", "ageclass", "Group")]) %>% 
  unique.data.frame()


p.djfm <- 
  ggplot(pred.model, aes(x = djfm, y = estimate, group = Group)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl,
                  ymax = ucl),
              alpha = 0.2) +
  
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  xlab("NAO index (DJFM)") +
  ylab("Survival probability \U03D5") +
  facet_wrap(~ageclass, ncol = 1,
             strip.position = "right") 
p.djfm

ggsave("plots/Fig_2_NAOEffect.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 4, height = 6, units = "in")
Condition
# create values of cond.index to use for predictions
cond.index.values = seq(from = min(df$cond), to = max(df$cond), length.out = 10)

## Extract get 'par.index' of interest
index.meta <- data.frame(
  Sex = rep(c("Female", "Male"), each = 4),
  ageclass = rep(c("CY1", "CY2", "CY3", "CY4plus"), 2)) %>% 
  ## add par.index
  left_join(., ddl$S[,c("par.index", "Sex", "ageclass")],
            by = c("Sex", "ageclass")) %>% 
  ## code additional vars
  mutate(dummy = paste0(Sex, ageclass)) 

## simplify, one par.index for each dummy var
model.indices <- sapply(unique(index.meta$dummy), function(x) {
  ## return first row matching the query
  index.meta[index.meta$dummy == x,"par.index"][1]
}) %>%
  unlist()

## subset index.meta
index.meta <- index.meta[index.meta$par.index %in% model.indices,]

## get estimates from the model
pred.model <- covariate.predictions(
  supported.models, 
  data = data.frame(
    cond = cond.index.values), 
  indices = model.indices %>% as.numeric(),
  drop = T)[["estimates"]]

## extend df with grouping variables
pred.model <- left_join(pred.model,
                        index.meta[,c("par.index", "ageclass", "Sex")], 
                        by = "par.index") %>%
  mutate(Group = paste0(Sex, ageclass))

## only showing estimates for females,  as sex-difference is small and additive, 
## whereby only the intercept but not the response varies
p.cond <- 
  ggplot(filter(pred.model, Sex ==  "Female"), aes(x = covdata, y = estimate, group = Group)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lcl,
                  ymax = ucl),
              alpha = 0.2) +
  
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  xlab("Standardised Body condition index") +
  ylab("Survival probability \U03D5") +
  facet_wrap(~ageclass, ncol = 1,
             strip.position = "right") + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) 
p.cond

ggsave("plots/Fig_3_BCI_Effect.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 4, height = 6, units = "in")
Leucocytozoon
leuco = 0:4

## Extract get par.index of interest
index.meta <- data.frame(
  ageclass = rep(c("CY1", "CY2", "CY3", "CY4plus"), 1)) %>%   
  ## add par.index
  left_join(., ddl$S[,c("par.index", "ageclass")]) %>% 
  ## code additional vars
  mutate(dummy = paste0(ageclass)) 

## simplify, one par.index for each dummy var
model.indices <- sapply(unique(index.meta$dummy), function(x) {
  ## return first row matching the query
  index.meta[index.meta$dummy == x,"par.index"][1]
}) %>% 
  unlist()

index.meta <- index.meta[index.meta$par.index %in% model.indices,]

pred.leuco <- covariate.predictions(
  supported.models, 
  data = data.frame(
    Lprev = leuco), 
  indices = index.meta$par.index)[["estimates"]]

## extend df with grouping variables
pred.model <- left_join(pred.leuco, index.meta[,c("par.index", "ageclass")], by = "par.index") %>% 
  mutate(Group = ageclass)

pred.model <- left_join(index.meta, pred.model[,c("par.index", "covdata", "estimate", "lcl", "ucl", "se", "ageclass", "Group")]) %>% 
  unique.data.frame()

p.leuco <- 
  ggplot(pred.model, aes(x = covdata, y = estimate, group = ageclass)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), width = 0, size = 0.75) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "Black", shape = 20) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  xlab("Leucozytozoon infection intensity") +
  ylab("Survival probability \U03D5 [mean \U00B1 CI]") +
  facet_wrap(~ageclass, ncol = 1,
             strip.position = "right")
p.leuco

ggsave("plots/Fig_4_Leucocytozoon_Effect.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 4, height = 6, units = "in")
Carnus
ekto = 0:4

## Extract get par.index of interest
index.meta <- data.frame(
  ageclass = rep(c("CY1", "CY2", "CY3", "CY4plus"), 1)) %>%   
  ## add par.index
  left_join(., ddl$S[,c("par.index", "ageclass")]) %>% 
  ## code additional vars
  mutate(dummy = paste0(ageclass)) 

## simplify, one par.index for each dummy var
model.indices <- sapply(unique(index.meta$dummy), function(x) {
  ## return first row matching the query
  index.meta[index.meta$dummy == x,"par.index"][1]
}) %>% 
  unlist()

index.meta <- index.meta[index.meta$par.index %in% model.indices,]

pred.ekto <- covariate.predictions(
  supported.models, 
  data = data.frame(
    Ekto = ekto), 
  indices = index.meta$par.index)[["estimates"]]

## extend df with grouping variables
pred.model <- left_join(pred.ekto, index.meta[,c("par.index", "ageclass")], by = "par.index") %>% 
  mutate(Group = ageclass)

pred.model <- left_join(index.meta, pred.model[,c("par.index", "covdata", "estimate", "lcl", "ucl", "ageclass", "Group")]) %>% 
  unique.data.frame()

p.ekto <- 
  ggplot(pred.model, aes(x = covdata, y = estimate, group = ageclass)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), width = 0, size = 0.75) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "Black", shape = 20) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,1),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  xlab("Ectoparasite infection intensity") +
  ylab("Survival probability \U03D5 [mean \U00B1 CI]") +
  facet_wrap(~ageclass, ncol = 1,
             strip.position = "right")

Resighting probabilies

All supported models (i.e. within \(\Delta QAIC_C <=10\)) contained a time-dependent component of the resighting probability, while a subset included an additive effect of Age (four classes).

## derive ageclass-specfic annual survival estimates
df.p.age <- lapply(rownames(p.mod.avg), function(row) {
  x <- stringr::str_split(row, " ")[[1]]
  data.frame(parameter = x[1],
             cohort = substr(x[3], 2, nchar(x[3])),
             year = as.numeric(substr(x[5], 2, nchar(x[5]))))
}) %>% 
  do.call("rbind",.) %>% 
  cbind(p.mod.avg) %>% 
  mutate(ageclass = case_when(
    age == 0 ~ 'CY1',
    age == 1 ~ 'CY2',
    age == 2 ~ 'CY3',
    age %in% 3:13 ~ 'CY4plus' ## 3:13 are identical 
  )) 

p.res <- 
  ggplot(filter(df.p.age, age %in% 1:3), aes(x = year, y = estimate, group = age)) +
  geom_line(lty = "dotted", aes(col = age)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - se, ymax = estimate +se,  color = age), width = 0, size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(col = age, shape = age), size = 2) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#FDB863", "#B2ABD2", "#5E3C99"),
                       labels = c("CY2", "CY3", "CY4+"),
                     name = "Age class") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(2, 5, 9),
                      labels = c("CY2", "CY3", "CY4+"),
                      name = "Age class") +
  #scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 0.6, 15)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2007, 2020, 1), limits = c(2007, 2020)) +
  xlab("Year") +
  ylab("Resighting probability \U03C1 [mean \U00B1 SE]")
p.res

ggsave("plots/Fig_1b_ResProb.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in")
## [1] 21.42877
## [1] 4.598892
## [1] 40.85527
## [1] 2.340036

Recovery probabilites

##  simplify model output
df.r.age <- lapply(rownames(r.mod.avg), function(row) {
  x <- stringr::str_split(row, " ")[[1]]
  data.frame(parameter = x[1],
             age = substr(x[4], 2, nchar(x[4])),
             year = as.numeric(substr(x[5], 2, nchar(x[5]))))
}) %>% 
  do.call("rbind",.) %>% 
  cbind(r.mod.avg) %>% 
  subset(., select = c(estimate, se, lcl, ucl, age)) 
df.r.age$age <- ifelse(df.r.age$age == "0", "CY1", "CY2+")

p.recov <- ggplot(data = df.r.age, aes(x = age, y = estimate)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), width = 0, size = 1, col = "Grey") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - se, ymax = estimate + se),
                width = 0, size = 2, col = "Grey") +
  geom_point(size = 3, shape = 19, col = "Black") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.3), breaks = seq(0, 0.3, 0.1)) +
  xlab("Age class") +
  ylab("Recovery probability r") 
p.recov

Fidelity (F)

All substantially supported models suggest either constant or sex-dependent survival probabilities, while only a minor fraction of all supported models includes a sex effect. Hence, for visualisation purposes, only one point estimate for each sex is displayed.

## estimates are essentially invariant based on model weights
## Female mean estimate
mean(F.mod.avg$estimate[F.mod.avg$Sex == "Female"])
## [1] 0.8900286
## variation
var(F.mod.avg$estimate[F.mod.avg$Sex == "Female"])
## [1] 6.657641e-07
F.mod.avg$SE = F.mod.avg$se

df.F <- data.frame(
  Sex = c("Female", "Male"),
  estimate = 
    DBChecks::summary_stats(F.mod.avg,
                            measurevar = "estimate",
                            groupvars = c("Sex"))[["estimate"]],
  lcl =
    DBChecks::summary_stats(F.mod.avg,
                            measurevar = "lcl",
                            groupvars = c("Sex"))[["lcl"]],
  ucl = 
    DBChecks::summary_stats(F.mod.avg,
                            measurevar = "ucl",
                            groupvars = c("Sex"))[["ucl"]],
  se = 
    DBChecks::summary_stats(F.mod.avg,
                            measurevar = "SE",
                            groupvars = c("Sex"))[["SE"]])

p.F <- ggplot(df.F, aes(x = Sex, y = estimate)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lcl, ymax = ucl), width = 0, size = 1, col = "Grey") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = estimate - se, ymax = estimate + se),
                width = 0, size = 2, col = "Grey") +
  geom_point(size = 3, shape = 19, col = "Black") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  xlab("Sex") +
  ylab("Site-fidelity F") 
p.F

ggsave("plots/Fig_1_AgeEffect.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in")

Multiplot

p.top <- p.age + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
              axis.title.x = element_blank(),
              axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
p.bot <- p.res
p.top / p.bot

ggsave("plots/Fig_1_S_Age_p_Age.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, 
       width = 6, height = 6, units = "in")

p.recov + p.F

ggsave("plots/Fig_7_F_and_r_estimates.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, 
       width = 6, height = 4, units = "in")

D.) Known cases of first year survival

## identify all cases where individuals died during the first year of life
dead <-
  filter(ringing_data,
         Dead == 'Yes',
         !CauseDeath %in% c('Jumper', 'Poisoned', 'Shot')) %>% 
  mutate(HatchingDate = as.Date(paste0(Year, "-01-01")) + Hatch) %>% 
  mutate(DaysSinceHatching = as.Date(DateDeath) - HatchingDate) %>% 
    filter(., DaysSinceHatching <= 365)

dead <- dead$Ring

## Identify all cases where individuals where alive after completing the first
## year of life, i.e. were resighted
alive <- resighting_data %>% 
  filter(DaysSinceHatching > 365)
alive <- alive$Ring %>% unique()

## merge to dataframe
df <- data.frame(Ring = c(dead, alive), 
                 Group = c(rep("Dead", length(dead)), rep("Alive", length(alive)))) %>% 
  left_join(., ringing_data, by = "Ring") %>% 
  mutate(surv = ifelse(Group == "Dead", 0, 1)) %>% 
  mutate(Leucocytozoon = ifelse(Lbinom == 0, "Uninfected", "Infected")) %>% 
  mutate(Carnus = ifelse(Ekto == 0, "Uninfected", "Infected")) %>% 
  mutate(Morph = case_when(
    Morph == "2" ~ "Dark",
    Morph == "3" ~ "Intermediate",
    Morph == "4" ~ "Light")) %>% 
  mutate(Hatch = scale(Hatch)) %>% 
  mutate(Age = scale(Age_days))

model <- glmer(
  surv ~ Leucocytozoon + cond + Sex + Carnus + Hatch + Age + (1|Year) ,
               data = df,
               family = "binomial")

model.plot <- plot_model(
  model = model,
  sort.est = TRUE,
  transform = NULL,
  title = "",
  ci.lvl = .95,
  ci.style = "whisker",
  dot.size = 3,
  line.size = 1.2) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = .7, color = "black"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.position = c(.8,.05),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA),
    panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA),
    text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = "dashed", alpha = .6) +
  ylab("\U03B2 estimate") + xlab("")
model.plot

ggsave("plots/Fig_2_KnownCases.tiff", device = "tiff", dpi = 300, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in")
tab_model(model,
          show.stat = T,
          string.est = "Estimate",
          string.ci = "95% CI",
          transform = NULL,
          collapse.ci = F)
  surv
Predictors Estimate 95% CI Statistic p
(Intercept) 1.48 0.87 – 2.09 4.78 <0.001
Leucocytozoon
[Uninfected] 		-0.05 -0.49 – 0.38 -0.23 0.821
cond 0.41 0.21 – 0.62 3.90 <0.001
Sex [Male] -0.18 -0.58 – 0.23 -0.84 0.400
Carnus [Uninfected] -0.46 -0.90 – -0.02 -2.03 0.043
Hatch -0.20 -0.46 – 0.05 -1.56 0.119
Age 0.06 -0.17 – 0.30 0.52 0.603
Random Effects
σ2 3.29
τ00 Year 0.44
ICC 0.12
N Year 12
Observations 583
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.062 / 0.172

Acknowledgements

The implemenation for calculating the adjusted \(\hat C\) via bootstrapping was based on a modified R code created by Michael J. Conroy (accessed from https://sites.google.com/site/cmrsoftware/lecture-lab-schedule/6---model-fit-and-mmi/methods-for-assessing-fit)

Appendix

Morph

Literature cited

  1. Hurrell, J.W., Kushnir, Y., Ottersen, G. & Visbeck, M. (2003). An overview of the North Atlantic oscillation. The North Atlantic Oscillation: climatic significance and environmental impact, 134, 1–35.↩︎
